title: foundation — base error envelope contract + universal codes
description: The error envelope shape every controller returns, plus the universal codes that don't belong to any specific module (401, 403, 404, 429, 500, ...). Downstream modules extend by adding module-specific codes with a namespace prefix (INVITATIONS_*, TENANCY_*, NOTIFICATIONS_*).

successResponse.shape: { data: <resource>, meta?: <paginator>, links?: <hateoas>, message?: <optional success toast> }
successResponse.notes: Success responses always wrap data. Empty responses use { data: null } rather than an empty body.

errorResponse.shape: { message: string, error: { code: string, http: integer, retryable: boolean, i18n_key: string }, errors?: { field: [strings] } }
notes: `message` — human-readable summary in the caller's locale (falls back to English).
`error.code` — machine-readable module-namespaced code. Always uppercase snake_case.
`error.http` — HTTP status echoed for clients that only see the body.
`error.retryable` — true if the client should retry with backoff (e.g. rate limits, transient failures).
`error.i18n_key` — dot-notation i18n key for FE translation lookup.
`errors` — per-field validation errors. Only present on 422.

envelopeContract.producedBy: Every ApiException subclass in every module → automatically wrapped by the `response.envelope` middleware.

errors.description: No valid token OR the token has expired. FE should redirect to sign-in.
Authenticated but lacks the required permission. FE should show a permission-denied panel.
Resource does not exist OR the caller does not have visibility to it (deliberate — do not distinguish to prevent enumeration).
Missing or invalid Accept header — api.version middleware rejects.
Optimistic-lock conflict or state-transition-forbidden. Module-specific codes should prefer their own.
Resource existed but is permanently removed.
Base validation failure. The `errors` field carries per-field messages.
Resource locked (e.g. account-lockout, tenant-suspended override). Module-specific codes preferred.
Rate limit exceeded. `Retry-After` header + `X-RateLimit-*` headers present. Module-specific codes (INVITATIONS_RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, etc.) preferred where the counter is specific.
Unhandled exception. Pages on-call. Response body never leaks stack trace or internals; the request_id is echoed so support can correlate with Sentry.
Route reserved for v2 (integrations, MFA on tenant guard, etc.). Deliberate to prevent probing.
Upstream (Stripe, SendGrid, SAML IdP) returned an error we can't recover from.
Kill-switch fired OR maintenance window active. `Retry-After` header set.
The ModuleRegistry expected a module that didn't discover. Deploy misconfiguration. Pages on-call.
Cycle detected in module dependency graph at boot. Fails startup.
A probe exceeded its declared timeout. HealthAggregator returned degraded/unhealthy.
The configured Scout engine (Meilisearch by default) is unreachable. Callers should degrade gracefully to filtered DB queries.
ConfigReloaded event fired with invalid config; downstream module rejected the reload. Rollback the config change.

downstreamConvention.codeFormat: MODULE_PREFIX_ERROR_KEY — uppercase snake_case with a module prefix (INVITATIONS_TOKEN_EXPIRED, TENANCY_CROSS_TENANT_ACCESS, NOTIFICATIONS_TEMPLATE_MISSING).
downstreamConvention.unique: Every code across every module is globally unique — the CI check flags duplicates.
downstreamConvention.audit_severity: info (nothing surprising), warn (unusual but expected — user error, transient failure), alert (pages on-call — indicates a bug or attack).
downstreamConvention.i18n_key: Must exist in every supported locale's message catalogue (en, ar, ...). Missing translations fall back to English.
